rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. QUAYLE TRUST DATED NOV. 11, 1980
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): 1) The trustee requests fees of $13,705 for his services during the account period, plus up to $2,500 for post-accounting services. 2) The trustee requests distribution of the cash of $27,261.87 on hand to Marky Quayle in partial satisfaction of the secured debt. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ADAM JOSEPH SIEPLE-MARGULIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/6/21. (No earlier availability.) Examiner Notes (issues necessitating continuance): A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his own pleadings (including any supplements or amendments thereto). [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] The order signed on 6/17/14 and filed on 6/18/14 permitted BAAM's 1% ANNUAL management fee and transaction costs but did not mention that they would be paid quarterly. Quarterly statements may need to be filed to support the quarterly payments. The "original" letter attached as Exhibit B to the attorney's declaration filed on 4/2/21 appears to be another computer-generated document. Requests to increase bond must be made immediately upon the occurrence of facts making it necessary or appropriate to increase the amount of bond (which this Court permits to be done via an ex parte petition), but requests to REDUCE bond will ordinarily only be granted when an accounting is filed to confirm that bond reduction is warranted. [LRSF 14.0, Appendix C.3., which refers to conservators but applies to any fiduciary in a protective proceeding.] Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/24/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Previously filed papers cannot simply be removed from a file upon request in a declaration. An ex parte petition must be filed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE JAIR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES: 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Petitioner has not filed a response to the following Examiner Notes: a. Objections allege notice was not given to Sharon So-Epprecht the alleged adult daughter of deceased sister Hazel So. Please explain why Sharon So-Epprecht is not listed on the petition page 4 under #8 and did not receive notice. Please explain if there are any other issue for Hazel So that must be listed on page 4 under #8. b. Please explain if there are any issue for Caroline Fong. (At the 3/10 hearing Carolyn Espey stated Hazel doesn't have any other children and Caroline Fong has no issue.) c. Petitioner Paul Perdue did not give notice to Carolyn Espey as requested in request for special notice filed 05/12/2017. (Carolyn Espey waived notice for the purpose of the 3/10/2021 hearing.) d. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] 3) Petitioner Paul Perdue has not filed a response to the objections. 4) Paul Perdue as attorney for Jack Jair alleged a supplemental I&A was filed showing cash of $2,039,727.47. No date for the filing was provided. No Supplemental I& has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENDRA MCGUINNESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/16/21. (No earlier availability.) Examiner Notes (issues necessitating continuance): Notice must be given to any other trust beneficiaries. [Probate Code Section 1208(b).] Is the executor the only trust beneficiary entitled to income or principal from the devisee trust? The petition and supplement are not entirely clear: did the executor liquidate the bonds or not? If he did, as the last sentence in paragraph 20 of the petition suggests, how is cash on hand exactly the same value as the assets on the date of death? If bonds have not been liquidated, they must be set forth in detail as assets to be distributed. [CRC 7.651(b).] Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/7/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
